description:
A colleague left a small data-conversion task for you in our web-app prototype directory.

    1. Locate the file  
       /home/user/project/assets/users.json  
       It already exists and contains a JSON array of user objects.

    2. Convert that JSON into a CSV file located at  
       /home/user/project/exports/users.csv  
       • The CSV **must include a single header row** exactly:  
         id,name,email  
       • Each subsequent row must list the values from every object in the JSON **in that same order**.  
       • Use a UNIX one-liner of your choice (e.g., jq, awk, etc.).  
       • The command should run from inside the container without requiring sudo privileges.

    3. No extra columns, no missing values, no surrounding quotation marks unless the field itself contains a comma.

    4. When you are done, cat the new CSV to the terminal so the output can be captured by our automated checker.

    Example (spacing added for clarity only):

        id,name,email
        1,Ada Lovelace,ada@example.com
        2,Linus Torvalds,linus@example.com
        3,Grace Hopper,grace@example.com

    Your final terminal output should therefore begin with the header line “id,name,email” followed by exactly one line per JSON object, in the same order they appear in the input file.
truth:
Initial file: /home/user/project/assets/users.json

    Contents (3 lines, compact, real file does not have extra whitespace):
    [
      {"id":1,"name":"Ada Lovelace","email":"ada@example.com"},
      {"id":2,"name":"Linus Torvalds","email":"linus@example.com"},
      {"id":3,"name":"Grace Hopper","email":"grace@example.com"}
    ]

    Target file: /home/user/project/exports/users.csv

    Correct final contents (4 lines, each ending in newline):
    id,name,email
    1,Ada Lovelace,ada@example.com
    2,Linus Torvalds,linus@example.com
    3,Grace Hopper,grace@example.com

    The automated test will:
    1. Assert that /home/user/project/exports/users.csv exists.
    2. Read the file and compare it byte-for-byte with the string above.
    3. Parse the agent’s terminal stdout for the identical 4-line CSV (including the header) printed via cat.
